title: warp-types: Type-Safe GPU Warp Programming via Linear Typestate
description: A type system that prevents shuffle-from-inactive-lane bugs in GPU warp programming by tracking active lane masks at compile time. Diverged warps literally cannot call shuffle — the method does not exist on the type. Zero runtime overhead verified at Rust MIR, LLVM IR, and NVIDIA PTX levels. Real GPU execution on NVIDIA RTX 4000 Ada; AMD MI300X mask-correctness verified via HIP. v0.3.0 adds 64-lane AMD wavefront support (--features warp64), C++20 interop header, and Lean 4 loop typing rules.
notes: Includes: Rust implementation (317 unit + 50 example + 28 doc = 395 tests), SimWarp multi-lane simulator, Lean 4 formalization (31 theorems, zero sorry, zero axioms — progress, preservation, all four §5.1 loop rules, nested merge, letPair), cargo-integrated GPU compilation pipeline (#[warp_kernel] + WarpBuilder), C++20 interop header (include/warp_types.h), CUB-equivalent typed warp primitives, and 8 worked bug examples covering 21 documented bugs across 16 real-world projects.
